title: Calculate Data Center PUE
description: Algorithm to calculate Power Usage Effectiveness for a data center
code:
ALGORITHM Calculate_PUE
INPUT: TotalFacilityEnergy (kWh), ITEquipmentEnergy (kWh)
OUTPUT: PUE_Value

BEGIN
 IF ITEquipmentEnergy = 0 THEN
 RETURN Error: IT energy cannot be zero
 END IF
 
 PUE_Value = TotalFacilityEnergy / ITEquipmentEnergy
 
 DISPLAY "PUE Value: " + PUE_Value
 
 IF PUE_Value <= 1.2 THEN
 DISPLAY "Excellent efficiency - World-class data center"
 ELSE IF PUE_Value <= 1.5 THEN
 DISPLAY "Good efficiency - Above average"
 ELSE IF PUE_Value <= 2.0 THEN
 DISPLAY "Average efficiency - Room for improvement"
 ELSE
 DISPLAY "Poor efficiency - Needs significant improvement"
 END IF
 
 RETURN PUE_Value
END
explanation: This algorithm calculates PUE by dividing total facility energy consumption by IT equipment energy consumption. A lower PUE indicates better efficiency, with 1.0 being ideal.

title: Energy-Efficient Server Consolidation
description: Algorithm to consolidate virtual machines on minimum servers
code:
ALGORITHM Server_Consolidation
INPUT: List_of_VMs, Server_Capacity
OUTPUT: VM_to_Server_Mapping

BEGIN
 Sort List_of_VMs by resource_requirements DESCENDING
 Initialize empty server_list
 current_server = Create_New_Server()
 
 FOR each VM in List_of_VMs DO
 IF Can_Fit_On_Server(current_server, VM) THEN
 Add VM to current_server
 ELSE
 Add current_server to server_list
 current_server = Create_New_Server()
 Add VM to current_server
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 Add current_server to server_list
 
 Calculate_Server_Utilization(server_list)
 Calculate_Energy_Savings(server_list)
 
 RETURN server_list
END
explanation: This algorithm performs server consolidation by sorting VMs by resource needs and packing them onto minimum servers, reducing energy consumption and improving utilization.

title: Carbon Footprint Calculator
description: Algorithm to calculate carbon emissions from IT operations
code:
ALGORITHM Calculate_Carbon_Footprint
INPUT: Energy_Consumption (kWh), Energy_Source_Type, Emission_Factor
OUTPUT: CO2_Emissions (kg)

BEGIN
 // Default emission factors (kg CO2 per kWh)
 Grid_Emission_Factor = 0.7
 Renewable_Emission_Factor = 0.05
 Diesel_Generator_Factor = 2.5
 
 IF Energy_Source_Type = "Grid" THEN
 Emission_Factor = Grid_Emission_Factor
 ELSE IF Energy_Source_Type = "Renewable" THEN
 Emission_Factor = Renewable_Emission_Factor
 ELSE IF Energy_Source_Type = "Diesel" THEN
 Emission_Factor = Diesel_Generator_Factor
 ELSE
 Emission_Factor = Emission_Factor // Use provided factor
 END IF
 
 CO2_Emissions = Energy_Consumption * Emission_Factor
 
 DISPLAY "Carbon Footprint: " + CO2_Emissions + " kg CO2"
 
 // Suggest reduction strategies
 IF CO2_Emissions > Threshold THEN
 DISPLAY "Recommendations:"
 DISPLAY "1. Switch to renewable energy sources"
 DISPLAY "2. Implement virtualization"
 DISPLAY "3. Improve cooling efficiency"
 DISPLAY "4. Use power management"
 END IF
 
 RETURN CO2_Emissions
END
explanation: This algorithm calculates CO2 emissions based on energy consumption and source type. Different energy sources have different emission factors, helping organizations track and reduce their carbon footprint.
